title: urostigma laevigatum
intro: Urostigma laevigatum, also known as the smooth-stalked stinging tree, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Asia and the Pacific Islands. The plant contains urticating hairs, which cause skin irritation and an allergic reaction upon contact, making it a defense mechanism against herbivores. The medicinal properties of Urostigma laevigatum include its potential anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it a traditional remedy for various health issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, and can be found in the form of tinctures, infusions, and poultices.
